1.1: On the first day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage), the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out) in the Tent of Meeting in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny). He said:
1.2: “Take a census of the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God) by their clans and families, listing every man by name, one by one.
1.3: You and Aaron (light bringer) are to number those who are twenty years of age or older by their divisions—everyone who can serve in Israel’s (he wrestles with God) army.
1.4: And one man from each tribe, the head of each family, must be there with you?.
1.5: These are the names of the men who are to assist you?: From the tribe of Reuben (behold a son), Elizur (my God is a rock) son of Shedeur (darter of light);
1.6: from Simeon (heard), Shelumiel (friend of God) son of Zurishaddai (my rock is almighty);
1.7: from Judah (praised), Nahshon (enchanter) son of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble);
1.8: from Issachar (there is recompense), Nethanel (given of God) son of Zuar (littleness);
1.9: from Zebulun (exalted), Eliab (God is father) son of Helon (strength);
1.10: from the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added): from Ephraim (doubly fruitful), Elishama (my God has heard) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty), and from Manasseh (causing to forget), Gamaliel (reward of God) son of Pedahzur (the Rock has ransomed);
1.11: from Benjamin (son of the right hand), Abidan (my father is judge) son of Gideoni (my hewer);
1.12: from Dan (He has judged), Ahiezer (my brother is help) son of Ammishaddai (my kinsman is Almighty);
1.13: from Asher (happy), Pagiel (event of God) son of Ocran (troubled);
1.14: from Gad (troop), Eliasaph (God has added) son of Deuel (they know God);
1.15: and from Naphtali (wrestling), Ahira (my brother is evil) son of Enan (having eyes).
1.16: These men were appointed from the congregation; they were the leaders of the tribes of their fathers, the heads of the clans of Israel (he wrestles with God).
1.17: So Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) took these men who had been designated by name,
1.18: and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation and recorded their ancestry by clans and families, counting one by one the names of those twenty years of age or older,
1.19: just as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out). So Moses numbered them in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny):
1.20: From the sons of Reuben (behold a son), the firstborn of Israel (he wrestles with God), according to the records of their clans and families, counting one by one the names of every male twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.21: those registered to the tribe of Reuben (behold a son) numbered 46,500.
1.22: From the sons of Simeon (heard), according to the records of their clans and families, counting one by one the names of every male twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.23: those registered to the tribe of Simeon (heard) numbered 59,300.
1.24: From the sons of Gad (troop), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.25: those registered to the tribe of Gad (troop) numbered 45,650.
1.26: From the sons of Judah (praised), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.27: those registered to the tribe of Judah (praised) numbered 74,600.
1.28: From the sons of Issachar (there is recompense), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.29: those registered to the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense) numbered 54,400.
1.30: From the sons of Zebulun (exalted), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.31: those registered to the tribe of Zebulun (exalted) numbered 57,400.
1.32: From the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added): From the sons of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.33: those registered to the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) numbered 40,500.
1.34: And from the sons of Manasseh (causing to forget), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.35: those registered to the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) numbered 32,200.
1.36: From the sons of Benjamin (son of the right hand), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.37: those registered to the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand) numbered 35,400.
1.38: From the sons of Dan (He has judged), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.39: those registered to the tribe of Dan (He has judged) numbered 62,700.
1.40: From the sons of Asher (happy), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.41: those registered to the tribe of Asher (happy) numbered 41,500.
1.42: From the sons of Naphtali (wrestling), according to the records of their clans and families, counting the names of all those twenty years of age or older who could serve in the army,
1.43: those registered to the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling) numbered 53,400.
1.44: These were the men numbered by Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer), with the assistance of the twelve leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God), each one representing his family.
1.45: So all the Israelites (God prevails) twenty years of age or older who could serve in Israel’s army were counted according to their families.
1.47: The Levites, however, were not numbered along with them by the tribe of their fathers.
1.48: For the LORD {YHWH} had said to Moses (drawn out):
1.49: “Do not number the tribe of Levi (joined to) in the census with the other Israelites (God prevails).
1.50: Instead, you are to appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the Testimony, all its furnishings, and everything in it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its articles, care for it, and camp around it.
1.51: Whenever the tabernacle is to move, the Levites are to take it down, and whenever it is to be pitched, the Levites are to set it up. Any outsider who goes near it must be put to death.
1.52: The Israelites (God prevails) are to camp by their divisions, each man in his own camp and under his own standard.
1.53: But the Levites are to camp around the tabernacle of the Testimony and watch over it, so that no wrath will fall on the congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God). So the Levites are responsible for the tabernacle of the Testimony.
1.54: Thus the Israelites (God prevails) did everything just as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).

2.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer):
2.2: “The Israelites (God prevails) are to camp around the Tent of Meeting at a distance from it, each man under his standard, with the banners of his family.
2.3: On the east side, toward the sunrise, the divisions of Judah (praised) are to camp under their standard: The leader of the Judahites is Nahshon (enchanter) son of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble),
2.5: The tribe of Issachar (there is recompense) will camp next to it. The leader of the Issacharites is Nethanel (given of God) son of Zuar (littleness),
2.7: Next will be the tribe of Zebulun (exalted). The leader of the Zebulunites is Eliab (God is father) son of Helon (strength),
2.9: The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Judah (praised) is 186,400; they shall set out first.
2.10: On the south side, the divisions of Reuben (behold a son) are to camp under their standard: The leader of the Reubenites is Elizur (my God is a rock) son of Shedeur (darter of light),
2.12: The tribe of Simeon (heard) will camp next to it. The leader of the Simeonites is Shelumiel (friend of God) son of Zurishaddai (my rock is almighty),
2.14: Next will be the tribe of Gad (troop). The leader of the Gadites is Eliasaph (God has added) son of Deuel [Reuel],
2.16: The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Reuben (behold a son) is 151,450; they shall set out second.
2.17: In the middle of the camps, the Tent of Meeting is to travel with the camp of the Levites. They are to set out in the order they encamped, each in his own place under his standard.
2.18: On the west side, the divisions of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) are to camp under their standard: The leader of the Ephraimites is Elishama (my God has heard) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty),
2.20: The tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) will be next to it. The leader of the Manassites is Gamaliel (reward of God) son of Pedahzur (the Rock has ransomed),
2.22: Next will be the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand). The leader of the Benjamites is Abidan (my father is judge) son of Gideoni (my hewer),
2.24: The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) is 108,100; they shall set out third.
2.25: On the north side, the divisions of Dan (He has judged) are to camp under their standard: The leader of the Danites is Ahiezer (my brother is help) son of Ammishaddai (my kinsman is Almighty),
2.27: The tribe of Asher (happy) will camp next to it. The leader of the Asherites is Pagiel (event of God) son of Ocran (troubled),
2.29: Next will be the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling). The leader of the Naphtalites is Ahira (my brother is evil) son of Enan (having eyes),
2.31: The total number of men in the camp of Dan (He has judged) is 157,600; they shall set out last, under their standards.
2.32: These are the Israelites (God prevails), numbered according to their families. The total of those counted in the camps, by their divisions, was 603,550.
2.33: But the Levites were not counted among the other Israelites (God prevails), as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).
2.34: So the Israelites (God prevails) did everything the LORD {YHWH} commanded Moses (drawn out); they camped under their standards in this way and set out in the same way, each man with his clan and his family.

3.1: This is the account of Aaron (light bringer) and Moses (drawn out) at the time the LORD {YHWH} spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai (thorny).
3.2: These are the names of the sons of Aaron (light bringer): Nadab (generous) the firstborn, then Abihu (he is my father), Eleazar (God has helped), and Ithamar (coast of palms).
3.3: These were Aaron’s (light bringer) sons, the anointed priests, who were ordained to serve as priests.
3.4: Nadab (generous) and Abihu, however (he is my father), died in the presence of the LORD {YHWH} when they offered unauthorized (strange) fire before the LORD in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny). And since they had no sons, only Eleazar (God has helped) and Ithamar (coast of palms) served as priests during the lifetime of their father Aaron (light bringer).
3.5: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
3.6: “Bring the tribe of Levi and present them to Aaron (light bringer) the priest to assist him.
3.7: They are to perform duties for him and for the whole congregation before the Tent of Meeting, attending to the service of the tabernacle.
3.8: They shall take care of all the furnishings of the Tent of Meeting and fulfill obligations for the Israelites (God prevails) by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
3.9: Assign the Levites to Aaron (light bringer) and his sons; they have been given exclusively to him [to Me] from among the Israelites (God prevails).
3.10: So you shall appoint Aaron (light bringer) and his sons to carry out the duties of the priesthood; but any outsider who approaches the tabernacle must be put to death.
3.11: Again the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out), saying,
3.12: “Behold, I have taken the Levites from among the children of Israel (he wrestles with God) in place of every firstborn Israelite from the womb. The Levites belong to Me,
3.13: for all the firstborn are Mine. On the day I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt (land of bondage), I consecrated to Myself all the firstborn in Israel (he wrestles with God), both man and beast. They are Mine; I am the LORD {YHWH}.
3.14: Then the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out) in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny), saying,
3.15: “Number the Levites (joined to) by their families and clans. You are to count every male a month old or more.
3.16: So Moses (drawn out) numbered them according to the word of the LORD {YHWH}, as he had been commanded.
3.17: These were the sons of Levi (joined to) by name: Gershon (exile), Kohath (assembly), and Merari (bitter).
3.18: These were the names of the sons of Gershon (exile) by their clans: Libni (white) and Shimei (renowned).
3.19: The sons of Kohath (assembly) by their clans were Amram (exalted people), Izhar (shining oil), Hebron (association), and Uzziel (my strength is God).
3.20: And the sons of Merari (bitter) by their clans were Mahli (sick) and Mushi (yielding). These were the clans of the Levites, according to their families.
3.21: From Gershon came (exile) the Libnite clan and the Shimeite clan; these were the Gershonite clans.
3.22: The number of all the males a month old or more was 7,500.
3.23: The Gershonite clans were to camp on the west, behind the tabernacle,
3.24: and the leader of the families of the Gershonites was Eliasaph (God has added) son of Lael (belonging to God).
3.25: The duties of the Gershonites (exile) at the Tent of Meeting were the tabernacle and tent, its covering, the curtain for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting,
3.26: the curtains of the courtyard, the curtain for the entrance to the courtyard that surrounds the tabernacle and altar, and the cords—all the service for these items.
3.27: From Kohath came (assembly) the clans of the Amramites, the Izharites (oil), the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites; these were the clans of the Kohathites.
3.28: The number of all the males a month old or more was 8,600 [8,300]. They were responsible for the duties of the sanctuary.
3.29: The clans of the Kohathites (assembly) were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle,
3.30: and the leader of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan (Elzaphan) son of Uzziel (my strength is God).
3.31: Their duties were the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the articles of the sanctuary used with them, and the curtain—all the service for these items.
3.32: The chief of the leaders of the Levites was Eleazar (God has helped) son of Aaron (light bringer) the priest; he oversaw those responsible for the duties of the sanctuary.
3.33: From Merari came the clans of the Mahlites and Mushites; these were the Merarite clans.
3.34: The number of all the males a month old or more was 6,200.
3.35: The leader of the families of the Merarites was Zuriel (my rock is God) son of Abihail (my father is might); they were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
3.36: The duties assigned to the sons of Merari (bitter) were the tabernacle’s frames, crossbars, posts, bases, and all its equipment—all the service for these items,
3.37: as well as the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases, tent pegs, and ropes.
3.38: Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and Aaron’s sons were to camp to the east of the tabernacle, toward the sunrise, before the Tent of Meeting. They were to perform the duties of the sanctuary as a service on behalf of the Israelites (God prevails); but any outsider who approached the sanctuary was to be put to death.
3.39: The total number of Levites that Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) counted by their clans at the LORD’s {YHWH} command, including all the males a month old or more, was 22,000.
3.40: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out), “Number every firstborn male of the Israelites (God prevails) a month old or more, and list their names.
3.41: You are to take the Levites for Me—I am the LORD {YHWH}—in place of all the firstborn of Israel (he wrestles with God), and the livestock of the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the livestock of the Israelites.
3.42: So Moses (drawn out) numbered all the firstborn of the Israelites (God prevails), as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded him.
3.43: The total number of the firstborn males a month old or more, listed by name, was 22,273.
3.44: Again the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out), saying,
3.45: “Take the Levites in place of all the firstborn of Israel (he wrestles with God), and the livestock of the Levites in place of their livestock. The Levites belong to Me; I am the LORD {YHWH}.
3.46: To redeem the 273 firstborn Israelites (God prevails) who outnumber the Levites,
3.47: you are to collect five shekels <2 oz / 57 g> for each one, according to the sanctuary shekel of twenty gerahs <0.4 oz / 11.4 g>.
3.48: Give the money to Aaron (light bringer) and his sons as the redemption price for the excess among the Israelites.
3.49: So Moses (drawn out) collected the redemption money from those in excess of the number redeemed by the Levites.
3.50: He collected the money from the firstborn of the Israelites (God prevails): 1,365 shekels <34.3 lb / 15.6 kg>, according to the sanctuary shekel.
3.51: And Moses (drawn out) gave the redemption money to Aaron (light bringer) and his sons in obedience to the word of the LORD {YHWH}, just as the LORD had commanded him.

4.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer),
4.2: “Take a census of the Kohathites (assembly) among the Levites (joined to) by their clans and families,
4.3: men from thirty to fifty years old—everyone who is qualified to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
4.4: This service of the Kohathites (assembly) at the Tent of Meeting regards the most holy things.
4.5: Whenever the camp sets out, Aaron (light bringer) and his sons are to go in, take down the veil of the curtain, and cover the ark of the Testimony (the ark of the covenant) with it.
4.6: They are to place over this a covering of fine leather, spread a solid blue cloth over it, and insert its poles.
4.7: Over the table of the Presence they are to spread a blue cloth and place the plates and cups on it, along with the bowls and pitchers for the drink offering. The regular bread offering is to remain on it.
4.8: And they shall spread a scarlet cloth over them, cover them with fine leather, and insert the poles.
4.9: They are to take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand used for light, together with its lamps, wick trimmers, and trays, as well as the jars of oil with which to supply it.
4.10: Then they shall wrap it and all its utensils inside a covering of fine leather and put it on the carrying frame.
4.11: Over the gold altar they are to spread a blue cloth, cover it with fine leather, and insert the poles.
4.12: They are to take all the utensils for serving in the sanctuary, place them in a blue cloth, cover them with fine leather, and put them on the carrying frame.
4.13: Then they shall remove the ashes from the bronze altar, spread a purple cloth over it,
4.14: and place on it all the vessels used to serve there: the firepans, meat forks, shovels, and sprinkling bowls—all the equipment of the altar. They are to spread over it a covering of fine leather and insert the poles.
4.15: When Aaron (light bringer) and his sons have finished covering the holy objects and all their equipment, as soon as the camp is ready to move, the Kohathites (assembly) shall come and do the carrying. But they must not touch the holy objects, or they will die. These are the transportation duties (burden) of the Kohathites regarding the Tent of Meeting.
4.16: Eleazar (God has helped) son of Aaron (light bringer) the priest shall oversee the oil for the light, the fragrant incense, the daily grain offering, and the anointing oil. He has oversight of the entire tabernacle and everything in it, including the holy objects and their utensils.
4.17: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer),
4.18: “Do not allow the Kohathite tribal clans to be cut off from among the Levites.
4.19: In order that they may live and not die when they come near the most holy things, do this for them: Aaron (light bringer) and his sons are to go in and assign each man his task and what he is to carry (burden).
4.20: But the Kohathites are not to go in and look at the holy objects, even for a moment, or they will die.
4.21: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
4.22: “Take a census of the Gershonites (exile) as well, by their families and clans,
4.23: from thirty to fifty years old, counting everyone who comes to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
4.24: This is the service of the Gershonite clans regarding work and transport (burden):
4.25: They are to carry the curtains of the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting with the covering of fine leather over it, the curtains for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting,
4.26: the curtains of the courtyard, and the curtains for the entrance at the gate of the courtyard that surrounds the tabernacle and altar, along with their ropes and all the equipment for their service. The Gershonites will do all that needs to be done with these items.
4.27: All the service of the Gershonites—all their transport duties (burden) and other work—is to be done at the direction of Aaron (light bringer) and his sons; you? are to assign to them all that they are responsible to carry.
4.28: This is the service of the Gershonite clans at the Tent of Meeting, and their duties shall be under the direction of Ithamar (coast of palms) son of Aaron (light bringer) the priest.
4.29: As for the sons of Merari (bitter), you are to number them by their clans and families,
4.30: from thirty to fifty years old, counting everyone who comes to serve in the work of the Tent of Meeting.
4.31: This is the duty for all their service at the Tent of Meeting: to carry (burden) the frames of the tabernacle with its crossbars, posts, and bases,
4.32: and the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases, tent pegs, and ropes, including all their equipment and everything related to their use. You? shall assign by name the items that they are responsible to carry (burden).
4.33: This is the service of the Merarite (bitter) clans according to all their work at the Tent of Meeting, under the direction of Ithamar (coast of palms) son of Aaron (light bringer) the priest.
4.34: So Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and the leaders of the congregation numbered the Kohathites by their clans and families,
4.35: everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
4.36: And those numbered by their clans totaled 2,750.
4.37: These were counted from the Kohathite clans, everyone who could serve at the Tent of Meeting. Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) numbered them according to the command of the LORD {YHWH} through Moses.
4.38: Then the Gershonites (exile) were numbered by their clans and families,
4.39: everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
4.40: And those numbered by their clans and families totaled 2,630.
4.41: These were counted from the Gershonite (exile) clans who served at the Tent of Meeting, whom Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) counted at the LORD’s {YHWH} command.
4.42: And the Merarites (bitter) were numbered by their clans and families,
4.43: everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
4.44: The men registered by their clans numbered 3,200.
4.45: These were counted from the Merarite (bitter) clans, whom Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) numbered at the LORD’s {YHWH} command through Moses.
4.46: So Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God) numbered by their clans and families all the Levites
4.47: from thirty to fifty years old who came to do the work of serving and carrying (burden) the Tent of Meeting.
4.48: And the number of men was 8,580.
4.49: At the LORD’s {YHWH} command through Moses (drawn out) they were numbered, and each one was assigned his work and burden (burden), as the LORD had commanded Moses.

5.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
5.2: “Command the Israelites (God prevails) to send away from the camp anyone with a skin disease {tzaraath}, anyone who has a bodily discharge, and anyone who is defiled by a dead body.
5.3: You? must send away male and female alike; send them outside the camp so they will not defile their camp, where I dwell among them.
5.4: So the Israelites (God prevails) did this, sending such people outside the camp. They did just as the LORD {YHWH} had instructed Moses (drawn out).
5.5: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
5.6: “Tell the Israelites that (he wrestles with God) when a man or woman acts unfaithfully against the LORD {YHWH} by committing any sin against another, that person is guilty
5.7: and must confess the sin he has committed. He must make full restitution, add a fifth to its value, and give all this to the one he has wronged.
5.8: But if the man has no relative to whom restitution can be made for the wrong, the restitution belongs to the LORD {YHWH} and must be given to the priest along with the ram of atonement, by which the atonement is made for him.
5.9: Every sacred contribution the Israelites (God prevails) bring to the priest shall belong to him.
5.10: Each man’s sacred gifts are his own, but whatever he gives to the priest will belong to the priest.
5.11: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
5.12: “Speak to the Israelites (God prevails) and tell them that if any man’s wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him
5.13: by sleeping with another man, and it is concealed from her husband and her impurity is undetected (since there is no witness against her and she was not caught in the act),
5.14: and if a feeling {ruach} of jealousy comes over her husband and he suspects his wife who has defiled herself—or if a feeling of jealousy comes over him and he suspects her even though she has not defiled herself—
5.15: then he is to bring his wife to the priest. He must also bring for her an offering of a tenth of an ephah of barley flour <2 qt / 2.2 l>. He is not to pour oil over it or put frankincense on it, because it is a grain offering for jealousy, an offering of memorial as a reminder of iniquity.
5.16: The priest is to bring the wife forward and have her stand before the LORD {YHWH}.
5.17: Then he is to take some holy water in a clay jar and put some of the dust from the tabernacle floor into the water.
5.18: After the priest has the woman stand before the LORD {YHWH}, he is to let down her hair and place in her hands the grain offering of memorial, which is the grain offering for jealousy. The priest is to hold the bitter water that brings a curse.
5.19: And he is to put the woman under oath and say to her, ‘If no other man has slept with you and you have not gone astray and become defiled while under your husband’s authority, may you be immune to this bitter water that brings a curse.
5.20: But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority and have defiled yourself and lain carnally with a man other than your husband’—
5.21: and the priest shall have the woman swear under the oath of the curse—‘then may the LORD {YHWH} make you an attested curse among your people by making your thigh shrivel and your belly swell.
5.22: May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach and cause your belly to swell and your thigh to shrivel. Then the woman is to say, ‘Amen, Amen.
5.23: And the priest shall write these curses on a scroll and wash them off into the bitter water.
5.24: He is to have the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse, and it will enter her and may cause her bitter suffering.
5.25: The priest shall take from her hand the grain offering for jealousy, wave it before the LORD {YHWH}, and bring it to the altar.
5.26: Then the priest is to take a handful of the grain offering as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar; after that he is to have the woman drink the water.
5.27: When he has made her drink the water, if she has defiled herself and been unfaithful to her husband, then the water that brings a curse will enter her and cause bitter suffering; her belly will swell, her thigh will shrivel, and she will become accursed among her people.
5.28: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, she will be unaffected and able to conceive children.
5.29: This is the law of jealousy when a wife goes astray and defiles herself while under her husband’s authority,
5.30: or when a feeling {ruach} of jealousy comes over a husband and he suspects his wife. He is to have the woman stand before the LORD {YHWH}, and the priest is to apply to her this entire law.
5.31: The husband will be free from guilt, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.

6.1: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
6.2: “Speak to the Israelites (God prevails) and tell them that if a man or woman makes a special vow, the vow of a Nazirite (one separated), to separate himself to the LORD {YHWH},
6.3: he is to abstain from wine and strong drink. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or strong drink, and he must not drink any grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins.
6.4: All the days of his separation, he is not to eat anything that comes from the grapevine, not even the seeds or skins.
6.5: For the entire period of his vow of separation, no razor shall touch his head. He must be holy until the time of his separation to the LORD {YHWH} is complete; he must let the hair of his head grow long.
6.6: Throughout the days of his separation to the LORD {YHWH}, he must not go near a dead body.
6.7: Even if his father or mother or brother or sister should die, he is not to defile himself, because the symbol of consecration to his God {Elohaw} is upon his head.
6.8: Throughout the time of his separation, he is holy to the LORD {YHWH}.
6.9: If someone suddenly dies in his presence and defiles his consecrated head of hair, he must shave his head on the day of his cleansing—the seventh day.
6.10: On the eighth day he must bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
6.11: And the priest is to offer one as a sin offering (purification offering) and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement for him, because he has sinned by being in the presence of the dead body. On that day he must consecrate his head again.
6.12: He must rededicate his time of separation to the LORD {YHWH} and bring a year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. But the preceding days shall not be counted, because his separation was defiled.
6.13: Now this is the law of the Nazirite when his time of separation is complete: He must be brought to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting,
6.14: and he is to present an offering to the LORD {YHWH} of an unblemished year-old male lamb as a burnt offering, an unblemished year-old female lamb as a sin offering, and an unblemished ram as a peace offering—
6.15: together with their grain offerings and drink offerings—and a basket of unleavened cakes made from fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers coated with oil.
6.16: The priest is to present all these before the LORD {YHWH} and make the sin offering and the burnt offering.
6.17: He shall also offer the ram as a peace offering to the LORD {YHWH}, along with the basket of unleavened bread. And the priest is to offer the accompanying grain offering and drink offering.
6.18: Then at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, the Nazirite is to shave his consecrated head, take the hair, and put it on the fire under the peace offering.
6.19: And the priest is to take the boiled shoulder from the ram, one unleavened cake from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and put them into the hands of the Nazirite who has just shaved the hair of his consecration.
6.20: The priest shall then wave them as a wave offering before the LORD {YHWH}. This is a holy portion for the priest, in addition to the breast of the wave offering and the thigh that was presented. After that, the Nazirite may drink wine.
6.21: This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to the LORD {YHWH} for his separation, in addition to whatever else he can afford; he must fulfill whatever vow he makes, according to the law of his separation.
6.22: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
6.23: “Tell Aaron (light bringer) and his sons: This is how you? are to bless the Israelites (God prevails). Say to them:
6.24: ‘May the LORD {YHWH} bless you and keep you;
6.25: may the LORD {YHWH} cause His face to shine upon you and be gracious to you;
6.26: may the LORD {YHWH} lift up His countenance toward you and give you peace.
6.27: So they shall put My name on the Israelites (God prevails), and I will bless them.

7.1: On the day Moses (drawn out) finished setting up the tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it and all its furnishings, along with the altar and all its utensils.
7.2: And the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God), the heads of their families, presented an offering. These men were the tribal leaders who had supervised the registration.
7.3: They brought as their offering before the LORD {YHWH} six covered carts and twelve oxen—an ox from each leader and a cart from every two leaders—and presented them before the tabernacle.
7.4: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
7.5: “Accept these gifts from them, that they may be used in the work of the Tent of Meeting. And give them to the Levites, to each man according to his service.
7.6: So Moses (drawn out) took the carts and oxen and gave them to the Levites.
7.7: He gave the Gershonites (exile) two carts and four oxen, as their service required,
7.8: and he gave the Merarites (bitter) four carts and eight oxen, as their service required, all under the direction of Ithamar (coast of palms) son of Aaron (light bringer) the priest.
7.9: But he did not give any to the Kohathites (assembly), since they were to carry on their shoulders the holy objects for which they were responsible.
7.10: When the altar was anointed, the leaders approached with their offerings for its dedication and presented them before the altar.
7.11: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out), “Each day one leader is to present his offering for the dedication of the altar.
7.12: On the first day Nahshon (enchanter) son of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble) from the tribe of Judah (praised) drew near with his offering.
7.13: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels <3.3 lb / 1.48 kg>, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels <1.76 lb / 797.8 g>, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.14: one gold dish weighing ten shekels <4 oz / 114 g>, filled with incense;
7.15: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.16: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.17: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahshon (enchanter) son of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble).
7.18: On the second day Nethanel (given of God) son of Zuar (littleness), the leader of Issachar (there is recompense), drew near.
7.19: The offering he presented was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.20: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.21: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.22: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.23: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nethanel (given of God) son of Zuar (littleness).
7.24: On the third day Eliab (God is father) son of Helon (strength), the leader of the Zebulunites, drew near (exalted).
7.25: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.26: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.27: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.28: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.29: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab (God is father) son of Helon (strength).
7.30: On the fourth day Elizur (my God is a rock) son of Shedeur (darter of light), the leader of the Reubenites, drew near (behold a son).
7.31: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.32: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.33: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.34: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.35: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur (my God is a rock) son of Shedeur (darter of light).
7.36: On the fifth day Shelumiel (friend of God) son of Zurishaddai (my rock is almighty), the leader of the Simeonites, drew near (heard).
7.37: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.38: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.39: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.40: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.41: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Shelumiel (friend of God) son of Zurishaddai (my rock is almighty).
7.42: On the sixth day Eliasaph (God has added) son of Deuel (they know God), the leader of the Gadites, drew near (troop).
7.43: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.44: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.45: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.46: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.47: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph (God has added) son of Deuel (they know God).
7.48: On the seventh day Elishama (my God has heard) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty), the leader of the Ephraimites, drew near (doubly fruitful).
7.49: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.50: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.51: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.52: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.53: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elishama (my God has heard) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty).
7.54: On the eighth day Gamaliel (reward of God) son of Pedahzur (the Rock has ransomed), the leader (causing to forget) of the Manassites, drew near.
7.55: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.56: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.57: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.58: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.59: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Gamaliel (reward of God) son of Pedahzur (the Rock has ransomed).
7.60: On the ninth day Abidan (my father is judge) son of Gideoni (my hewer), the leader of the Benjamites, drew near (son of the right hand).
7.61: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.62: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.63: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.64: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.65: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Abidan (my father is judge) son of Gideoni (my hewer).
7.66: On the tenth day Ahiezer (my brother is help) son of Ammishaddai (my kinsman is Almighty), the leader of the Danites, drew near (He has judged).
7.67: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.68: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.69: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.70: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.71: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahiezer (my brother is help) son of Ammishaddai (my kinsman is Almighty).
7.72: On the eleventh day Pagiel (event of God) son of Ocran (troubled), the leader of the Asherites, drew near (happy).
7.73: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.74: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.75: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.76: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.77: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Pagiel (event of God) son of Ocran (troubled).
7.78: On the twelfth day Ahira (my brother is evil) son of Enan (having eyes), the leader of the Naphtalites, drew near (wrestling).
7.79: His offering was one silver platter weighing a hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel and filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
7.80: one gold dish weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
7.81: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old for a burnt offering;
7.82: one male goat for a sin offering;
7.83: and a peace offering of two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahira (my brother is evil) son of Enan (having eyes).
7.84: So these were the offerings from the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God) for the dedication of the altar when it was anointed: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, and twelve gold dishes.
7.85: Each silver platter weighed a hundred and thirty shekels, and each silver bowl seventy shekels. The total weight of the silver articles was two thousand four hundred shekels <60.3 lb / 27.4 kg>, according to the sanctuary shekel.
7.86: The twelve gold dishes filled with incense weighed ten shekels each, according to the sanctuary shekel. The total weight of the gold dishes was a hundred and twenty shekels <3 lb / 1.4 kg>.
7.87: All the livestock for the burnt offering totaled twelve bulls, twelve rams, and twelve male lambs a year old—together with their grain offerings—and twelve male goats for the sin offering.
7.88: All the livestock sacrificed for the peace offering totaled twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, and sixty male lambs a year old. This was the dedication offering for the altar after it was anointed.
7.89: When Moses (drawn out) entered the Tent of Meeting to speak with the LORD, he heard the voice speaking to him from between the two cherubim above the mercy seat (atonement cover) on the ark of the Testimony (the ark of the covenant). Thus the LORD spoke to him.

8.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
8.2: “Speak to Aaron (light bringer) and tell him: ‘When you set up the seven lamps, they are to light the area in front of the lampstand.
8.3: And Aaron (light bringer) did so; he set up the lamps facing toward the front of the lampstand, just as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).
8.4: This is how the lampstand was constructed: it was made of hammered gold from its base to its blossoms, fashioned according to the pattern the LORD {YHWH} had shown Moses (drawn out).
8.5: Again the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out), saying,
8.6: “Take the Levites from among the Israelites (God prevails) and make them ceremonially clean.
8.7: This is what you must do to cleanse them: Sprinkle them with the water of purification. Have them shave their whole bodies and wash their clothes, and so purify themselves.
8.8: Then have them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you are to take a second young bull for a sin offering.
8.9: Bring the Levites before the Tent of Meeting and assemble the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God).
8.10: You are to present the Levites before the LORD {YHWH} and have the Israelites (God prevails) lay their hands upon them.
8.11: Aaron (light bringer) is to present the Levites before the LORD {YHWH} as a wave offering from the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God), so that they may perform the service of the LORD.
8.12: And the Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and offer to the LORD {YHWH} one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, to make atonement for the Levites.
8.13: You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron (light bringer) and his sons and then present them before the LORD {YHWH} as a wave offering.
8.14: In this way you shall separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites (God prevails) and the Levites will belong to Me.
8.15: After you have cleansed them and presented them as a wave offering, they may come to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
8.16: For the Levites have been wholly given to Me from among the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God). I have taken them for Myself in place of all who come first from the womb, the firstborn of all the sons of Israel.
8.17: For every firstborn male in Israel (he wrestles with God) is Mine, both man and beast. I set them apart for Myself on the day I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt (land of bondage).
8.18: But I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God).
8.19: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron (light bringer) and his sons from among the Israelites (God prevails), to perform the service for the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting and to make atonement on their behalf, so that no plague will come against the Israelites when they approach the sanctuary.
8.20: So Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God) did with the Levites everything that the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses they should do.
8.21: The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes, and Aaron (light bringer) presented them as a wave offering before the LORD {YHWH}. Aaron also made atonement for them to cleanse them.
8.22: After that, the Levites came to perform their service at the Tent of Meeting in the presence of Aaron (light bringer) and his sons. Thus they did with the Levites just as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).
8.23: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
8.24: “This applies to the Levites: Men twenty-five years of age or older shall enter to perform the service in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
8.25: But at the age of fifty, they must retire from performing the work and no longer serve.
8.26: After that, they may assist their brothers in fulfilling their duties at the Tent of Meeting, but they themselves are not to do the work. This is how you are to assign responsibilities to the Levites.

9.1: In the first month of the second year after Israel had come out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage), the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out) in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny):
9.2: “The Israelites (God prevails) are to observe the Passover at its appointed time.
9.3: You? are to observe it at the appointed time, at twilight on the fourteenth \between the two evenings of the fourteenth/ day of this month, in accordance with its statutes and ordinances.
9.4: So Moses (drawn out) told the Israelites (God prevails) to observe the Passover,
9.5: and they did so in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny), at twilight on the fourteenth day of the first month. The Israelites (God prevails) did everything just as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).
9.6: But there were some men who were unclean due to a dead body, so they could not observe the Passover on that day. And they came before Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) that same day
9.7: and said to Moses, “We are unclean because of a dead body, but why should we be excluded from presenting the LORD’s {YHWH} offering with the other Israelites (God prevails) at the appointed time?
9.8: “Wait here until I find out what the LORD {YHWH} commands concerning you?, Moses (drawn out) replied.
9.9: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
9.10: “Tell the Israelites (God prevails): ‘When any one of you? or your? descendants is unclean because of a dead body, or is away on a journey, he may still observe the Passover to the LORD {YHWH}.
9.11: Such people are to observe it at twilight on the fourteenth day of the second month. They are to eat the lamb, together with unleavened bread and bitter herbs;
9.12: they may not leave any of it until morning or break any of its bones. They must observe the Passover according to all its statutes.
9.13: But if a man who is ceremonially clean and is not on a journey still fails to observe the Passover, he must be cut off from his people, because he did not present the LORD’s {YHWH} offering at its appointed time. That man will bear the consequences of his sin.
9.14: If a foreigner dwelling among you? wants to observe the Passover to the LORD {YHWH}, he is to do so according to the Passover statute and its ordinances. You? are to apply the same statute to both the foreigner and the native of the land.
9.15: On the day that the tabernacle, the Tent of the Testimony, was set up, the cloud covered it and appeared like fire above the tabernacle from evening until morning.
9.16: It remained that way continually; the cloud would cover the tabernacle by day, and at night it would appear like fire.
9.17: Whenever the cloud was lifted from above the Tent, the Israelites (God prevails) would set out, and wherever the cloud settled, there the Israelites would camp.
9.18: At the LORD’s {YHWH} command the Israelites (God prevails) set out, and at the LORD’s command they camped. As long as the cloud remained over the tabernacle, they remained encamped.
9.19: Even when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle for many days, the Israelites (God prevails) kept the LORD’s {YHWH} charge and did not set out.
9.20: Sometimes the cloud remained over the tabernacle for only a few days, and they would camp at the LORD’s {YHWH} command and set out at the LORD’s command.
9.21: Sometimes the cloud remained only from evening until morning, and when it lifted in the morning, they would set out. Whether it was by day or by night, when the cloud was taken up, they would set out.
9.22: Whether the cloud lingered for two days, a month, or longer, the Israelites (God prevails) camped and did not set out as long as the cloud remained over the tabernacle; but when it was lifted, they would set out.
9.23: They camped at the LORD’s {YHWH} command, and they set out at the LORD’s command; they carried out the LORD’s charge according to His command through Moses (drawn out).

10.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
10.2: “Make two trumpets of hammered silver to be used for calling the congregation and for having the camps set out.
10.3: When both are sounded, the whole congregation is to assemble before you at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
10.4: But if only one is sounded, then the leaders, the heads of the clans of Israel (he wrestles with God), are to gather before you.
10.5: When you? sound short blasts, the camps that lie on the east side are to set out.
10.6: When you? sound the short blasts a second time, the camps that lie on the south side are to set out. The blasts are to signal them to set out.
10.7: To convene the assembly, you? are to sound long blasts, not short ones.
10.8: The sons of Aaron (light bringer), the priests, are to sound the trumpets. This shall be a permanent statute for you? and the generations to come.
10.9: When you? enter into battle in your? land against an adversary who attacks you?, sound short blasts on the trumpets, and you? will be remembered before the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} and saved from your? enemies.
10.10: And on your? joyous occasions, your? appointed feasts, and the beginning of each month, you? are to blow the trumpets over your? burnt offerings and peace offerings to serve as a reminder for you? before your? God {Eloheikem}. I am the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}.
10.11: On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year, the cloud was lifted from above the tabernacle of the Testimony,
10.12: and the Israelites (God prevails) set out from the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny), traveling from place to place until the cloud settled in the Wilderness of Paran (place of caverns).
10.13: They set out this first time according to the LORD’s {YHWH} command through Moses (drawn out).
10.14: First, the divisions of the camp of Judah (praised) set out under their standard, with Nahshon (enchanter) son of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble) in command.
10.15: Nethanel (given of God) son of Zuar (littleness) was over the division of the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense),
10.16: and Eliab (God is father) son of Helon (strength) was over the division of the tribe of Zebulun (exalted).
10.17: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites (exile) and the Merarites (bitter) set out, transporting it.
10.18: Then the divisions of the camp of Reuben (behold a son) set out under their standard, with Elizur (my God is a rock) son of Shedeur (darter of light) in command.
10.19: Shelumiel (friend of God) son of Zurishaddai (my rock is almighty) was over the division of the tribe of Simeon (heard),
10.20: and Eliasaph (God has added) son of Deuel (they know God) was over the division of the tribe of Gad (troop).
10.21: Then the Kohathites set out, transporting the holy objects; the tabernacle was to be set up before their arrival.
10.22: Next, the divisions of the camp of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) set out under their standard, with Elishama (my God has heard) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty) in command.
10.23: Gamaliel (reward of God) son of Pedahzur (the Rock has ransomed) was over the division of the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget),
10.24: and Abidan (my father is judge) son of Gideoni (my hewer) was over the division of the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand).
10.25: Finally, the divisions of the camp of Dan (He has judged) set out under their standard, serving as the rear guard for all units, with Ahiezer (my brother is help) son of Ammishaddai (my kinsman is Almighty) in command.
10.26: Pagiel (event of God) son of Ocran (troubled) was over the division of the tribe of Asher (happy),
10.27: and Ahira (my brother is evil) son of Enan (having eyes) was over the division of the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling).
10.28: This was the order of march for the Israelite (he wrestles with God) divisions as they set out.
10.29: Then Moses (drawn out) said to Hobab (cherished), the son of Moses’ father-in-law Reuel (Jethro) the Midianite, “We are setting out for the place of which the LORD {YHWH} said: ‘I will give it to you?. Come with us, and we will treat you well, for the LORD has promised good things to Israel (he wrestles with God).
10.30: “I will not go, Hobab replied. “Instead, I am going back to my own land and my own people.
10.31: “Please do not leave us, Moses said, “since you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you can serve as our eyes.
10.32: If you come with us, we will share with you whatever good things the LORD {YHWH} gives us.
10.33: So they set out on a three-day journey from the mountain of the LORD {YHWH} with the ark of the covenant of the LORD traveling ahead of them for those three days to seek a resting place for them.
10.34: And the cloud of the LORD {YHWH} was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
10.35: Whenever the ark set out, Moses (drawn out) would say, “Rise up, O LORD {YHWH}! May Your enemies be scattered; may those who hate You flee before You.
10.36: And when it came to rest, he would say: “Return, O LORD {YHWH}, to the countless thousands of Israel (he wrestles with God).

11.1: Soon the people began to complain about their hardship in the hearing of the LORD {YHWH}, and when He heard them, His anger was kindled, and fire from the LORD blazed among them and consumed the outskirts of the camp.
11.2: And the people cried out to Moses (drawn out), and he prayed to the LORD {YHWH}, and the fire died down.
11.3: So that place was called Taberah (burning), because the fire of the LORD {YHWH} had burned among them.
11.4: Meanwhile, the rabble among them had a strong craving for other food, and again the Israelites (God prevails) wept and said, “Who will feed us meat?
11.5: We remember the fish we ate freely in Egypt (land of bondage), along with the cucumbers, melons, leeks, onions, and garlic.
11.6: But now our appetite is gone; there is nothing to see but this manna!
11.7: Now the manna resembled coriander seed, and its appearance was like that of gum resin.
11.8: The people walked around and gathered it, ground it on a handmill or crushed it in a mortar, then boiled it in a cooking pot or shaped it into cakes. It tasted like pastry baked with fine oil.
11.9: When the dew fell on the camp at night, the manna would fall with it.
11.10: Then Moses (drawn out) heard the people of family after family weeping at the entrances to their tents, and the anger of the LORD {YHWH} was kindled greatly, and Moses was also displeased.
11.11: So Moses (drawn out) asked the LORD {YHWH}, “Why have You brought this trouble on Your servant? Why have I not found favor in Your sight, that You have laid upon me the burden (burden) of all these people?
11.12: Did I conceive all these people? Did I give them birth, so that You should tell me, ‘Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries an infant, to the land that You swore to give their fathers?
11.13: Where can I get meat for all these people? For they keep crying out to me, ‘Give us meat to eat!
11.14: I cannot carry all these people by myself; it is too burdensome for me.
11.15: If this is how You are going to treat me, please kill me right now—if I have found favor in Your eyes—and let me not see my own wretchedness.
11.16: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out), “Bring Me seventy of the elders of Israel (he wrestles with God) known to you as leaders and officers of the people. Bring them to the Tent of Meeting and have them stand there with you.
11.17: And I will come down and speak with you there, and I will take some of the Spirit {Ruach} that is on you and put that Spirit on them. They will help you bear the burden (burden) of the people, so that you do not have to bear it by yourself.
11.18: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you? will eat meat, because you? have cried out in the hearing of the LORD {YHWH}, saying: ‘Who will feed us meat? For we were better off in Egypt (land of bondage)! Therefore the LORD will give you? meat, and you? will eat.
11.19: You? will eat it not for one or two days, nor for five or ten or twenty days,
11.20: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your? nostrils and makes you? nauseous—because you? have rejected the LORD {YHWH}, who is among you?, and have cried out before Him, saying, ‘Why did we ever leave Egypt (land of bondage)?
11.21: But Moses (drawn out) replied, “Here I am among 600,000 men on foot, yet You say, ‘I will give them meat, and they will eat for a month.
11.22: If all our flocks and herds were slaughtered for them, would they have enough? Or if all the fish in the sea were caught for them, would they have enough?
11.23: The LORD {YHWH} answered Moses (drawn out), “Is the LORD’s arm too short? Now you will see whether or not My word will come to pass.
11.24: So Moses (drawn out) went out and relayed to the people the words of the LORD {YHWH}, and he gathered seventy of the elders of the people and had them stand around the tent.
11.25: Then the LORD {YHWH} came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and He took some of the Spirit {Ruach} that was on Moses and placed that Spirit on the seventy elders. As the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied—but they never did so again.
11.26: Two men, however, had remained in the camp—one named Eldad (God has loved) and the other Medad (love)—and the Spirit {Ruach} rested on them. They were among those listed, but they had not gone out to the tent, and they prophesied in the camp.
11.27: A young man ran and reported to Moses (drawn out), “Eldad (God has loved) and Medad (love) are prophesying in the camp.
11.28: Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), the attendant to Moses (drawn out) since youth, spoke up and said, “Moses, my lord, stop them!
11.29: But Moses (drawn out) replied, “Are you jealous on my account? I wish that all the LORD’s {YHWH} people were prophets and that the LORD would place His Spirit {Ruachow} on them!
11.30: Then Moses (drawn out) returned to the camp, along with the elders of Israel (he wrestles with God).
11.31: Now a wind {ruach} sent by the LORD {YHWH} came up, drove in quail from the sea, and brought them near the camp, about two cubits above the surface of the ground <3 ft / 91.4 cm>, for a day’s journey in every direction around the camp.
11.32: All that day and night, and all the next day, the people stayed up gathering the quail. No one gathered less than ten homers <62.4 bu / 2,200 l>, and they spread them out all around the camp.
11.33: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it was chewed, the anger of the LORD {YHWH} burned against the people, and the LORD struck them with a severe plague.
11.34: So they called that place Kibroth-hattaavah (graves of craving), because there they buried the people who had craved other food.
11.35: From Kibroth-hattaavah (graves of craving) the people moved on to Hazeroth (settlement), where they remained for some time.

12.1: Then Miriam (rebellion) and Aaron (light bringer) criticized Moses (drawn out) because of the Cushite woman he had married, for he had taken a Cushite wife.
12.2: “Does the LORD {YHWH} speak only through Moses (drawn out)? they said. “Does He not also speak through us? And the LORD heard this.
12.3: Now Moses (drawn out) was a very humble man, more so than any man on the face of the earth.
12.4: And suddenly the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and Miriam (rebellion), “You? three, come out to the Tent of Meeting. So the three went out,
12.5: and the LORD {YHWH} came down in a pillar of cloud, stood at the entrance to the Tent, and summoned Aaron (light bringer) and Miriam (rebellion). When both of them had stepped forward,
12.6: He said, “Hear now My words: If there is a prophet among you?, I, the LORD {YHWH}, will reveal Myself to him in a vision; I will speak to him in a dream.
12.7: But this is not so with My servant Moses (drawn out); he is faithful in all My house .
12.8: I speak with him face to face, clearly and not in riddles; he sees the form of the LORD {YHWH}. Why then were you? unafraid to speak against My servant Moses (drawn out)?
12.9: So the anger of the LORD {YHWH} burned against them, and He departed.
12.10: As the cloud lifted from above the Tent, suddenly Miriam (rebellion) became leprous, white as snow. Aaron (light bringer) turned toward her, saw that she was leprous,
12.11: and said (light bringer) to Moses (drawn out), “My lord, please do not hold against us this sin we have so foolishly committed.
12.12: Please do not let her be like a stillborn infant whose flesh is half consumed when he comes out of his mother’s womb.
12.13: So Moses (drawn out) cried out to the LORD {YHWH}, “O God, please heal her!
12.14: But the LORD {YHWH} answered Moses (drawn out), “If her father had but spit in her face, would she not have been in disgrace for seven days? Let her be confined outside the camp for seven days; after that she may be brought back in.
12.15: So Miriam (rebellion) was confined outside the camp for seven days, and the people did not move on until she was brought in again.
12.16: After that, the people set out from Hazeroth (settlement) and camped in the Wilderness of Paran (place of caverns).

13.1: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
13.2: “Send out for yourself men to spy out the land of Canaan (lowland), which I am giving to the Israelites (God prevails). From each of their fathers’ tribes send one man who is a leader among them.
13.3: So at the command \according to the mouth/ of the LORD {YHWH}, Moses (drawn out) sent them out from the Wilderness of Paran (place of caverns). All the men were leaders of the Israelites (God prevails),
13.4: and these were their names: From the tribe of Reuben (behold a son), Shammua (renowned) son of Zaccur (mindful);
13.5: from the tribe of Simeon (heard), Shaphat (He has judged) son of Hori (cave dweller);
13.6: from the tribe of Judah (praised), Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing);
13.7: from the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense), Igal (He redeems) son of Joseph (YHWH has added);
13.8: from the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), Hoshea (salvation) son of Nun (fish / posterity);
13.9: from the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand), Palti (my deliverance) son of Raphu (healed);
13.10: from the tribe of Zebulun (exalted), Gaddiel (God is my fortune) son of Sodi (acquaintance);
13.11: from the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) (a tribe of Joseph (YHWH has added)), Gaddi (my fortune) son of Susi (my horse);
13.12: from the tribe of Dan (He has judged), Ammiel (my kinsman is God) son of Gemalli (camel driver);
13.13: from the tribe of Asher (happy), Sethur (hidden) son of Michael (who is like God);
13.14: from the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling), Nahbi (hidden) son of Vophsi (rich);
13.15: and from the tribe of Gad (troop), Geuel (majesty of God) son of Machi (decrease).
13.16: These were the names of the men Moses (drawn out) sent to spy out the land; and Moses gave to Hoshea (salvation) son of Nun (fish / posterity) the name Joshua (YHWH saves).
13.17: When Moses (drawn out) sent them to spy out the land of Canaan (lowland), he told them, “Go up through the Negev (southland) and into the hill country.
13.18: See what the land is like and whether its people are strong or weak, few or many.
13.19: Is the land where they live good or bad? Are the cities where they dwell open camps or fortifications?
13.20: Is the soil fertile or unproductive? Are there trees in it or not? Be courageous and bring back some of the fruit of the land. (It was the season for the first ripe grapes.
13.21: So they went up and spied out the land from the Wilderness of Zin (flat) as far as Rehob (broad place), toward Lebo-hamath (fortress).
13.22: They went up through the Negev (southland) and came to Hebron (association), where Ahiman (my brother is a gift), Sheshai (noble), and Talmai (furrowed), the descendants of Anak (neck), dwelled. It had been built seven years before Zoan (place of departure) in Egypt (land of bondage).
13.23: When they came to the Valley of Eshcol (cluster), they cut down a branch with a single cluster of grapes, which they carried on a pole between two men. They also took some pomegranates and figs.
13.24: Because of the cluster of grapes the Israelites (God prevails) cut there, that place was called the Valley of Eshcol (cluster).
13.25: After forty days the men returned from spying out the land,
13.26: and they went back to Moses (drawn out), Aaron (light bringer), and the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God) in the Wilderness of Paran (place of caverns) at Kadesh (holy). They brought back a report for the whole congregation and showed them the fruit of the land.
13.27: And they gave this account to Moses: “We went into the land to which you sent us, and indeed, it is flowing with milk and honey. Here is some of its fruit!
13.28: Nevertheless, the people living in the land are strong, and the cities are large and fortified. We even saw the descendants of Anak (neck) there.
13.29: The Amalekites (dwellers in a valley) live in the land of the Negev (southland); the Hittites, Jebusites (Jerusalemites), and Amorites (sayers) live in the hill country; and the Canaanites (merchants) live by the sea and along the Jordan (flowing down).
13.30: Then Caleb (dog) quieted the people before Moses (drawn out) and said, “We must go up and take possession of the land, for we can certainly conquer it!
13.31: But the men who had gone up with him replied, “We cannot go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are!
13.32: So they gave the Israelites a bad report (he wrestles with God) about the land that they had spied out: “The land we explored devours its inhabitants, and all the people we saw there are great in stature.
13.33: We even saw the Nephilim (giants) there—the descendants of Anak (neck) that come from the Nephilim! We seemed like grasshoppers in our own sight, and we must have seemed the same to them!

14.1: Then the whole congregation lifted up their voices and cried out, and that night the people wept.
14.2: All the Israelites (God prevails) grumbled against Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer), and the whole congregation said to them, “If only we had died in the land of Egypt (land of bondage), or if only we had died in this wilderness!
14.3: Why is the LORD {YHWH} bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and children will become plunder. Would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt (land of bondage)?
14.4: So they said to one another, “Let us appoint a leader and return to Egypt (land of bondage).
14.5: Then Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer) fell facedown before the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God).
14.6: Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) and Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing), who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
14.7: and said to the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God), “The land we passed through and explored is an exceedingly good land.
14.8: If the LORD {YHWH} delights in us, He will bring us into this land, a land flowing with milk and honey, and He will give it to us.
14.9: Only do not rebel against the LORD {YHWH}, and do not be afraid of the people of the land, for they will be like bread for us. Their protection has been removed, and the LORD is with us. Do not be afraid of them!
14.10: But the whole congregation threatened to stone Joshua and Caleb. Then the glory of the LORD {YHWH} appeared to all the Israelites (God prevails) at the Tent of Meeting.
14.11: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out), “How long will this people treat Me with contempt? How long will they refuse to believe in Me, despite all the signs I have performed among them?
14.12: I will strike them with a plague and destroy them—and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than they are.
14.13: But Moses (drawn out) said to the LORD {YHWH}, “The Egyptians (double straits) will hear of it, for by Your strength You brought this people from among them.
14.14: And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land. They have already heard that You, O LORD {YHWH}, are in the midst of this people, that You, O LORD, have been seen face to face, that Your cloud stands over them, and that You go before them in a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.
14.15: If You kill this people as one man, the nations who have heard of Your fame will say,
14.16: ‘Because the LORD {YHWH} was unable to bring this people into the land He swore to give them, He has slaughtered them in the wilderness.
14.17: So now I pray, may the power of my Lord {Adonai} be magnified, just as You have declared:
14.18: ‘The LORD {YHWH} is slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion {chesed}, forgiving iniquity and transgression. Yet He will by no means leave the guilty unpunished; He will visit the iniquity of the fathers upon their children to the third and fourth generation.
14.19: Pardon, I pray, the iniquity of this people, in keeping with the greatness of Your loving devotion, just as You have forgiven them ever since they left Egypt (land of bondage).
14.20: “I have pardoned them as you requested, the LORD {YHWH} replied.
14.21: “Yet as surely as I live and as surely as the whole earth is filled with the glory of the LORD {YHWH},
14.22: not one of the men who have seen My glory and the signs I performed in Egypt (land of bondage) and in the wilderness—yet have tested Me and disobeyed Me these ten times—
14.23: not one will ever see the land that I swore to give their fathers. None of those who have treated Me with contempt will see it.
14.24: But because My servant Caleb (dog) has a different spirit {ruach} and has followed Me wholeheartedly, I will bring him into the land he has entered, and his descendants will inherit it.
14.25: Now since the Amalekites (dwellers in a valley) and Canaanites (merchants) are living in the valleys, turn back tomorrow and head for the wilderness along the route to the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds).
14.26: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer),
14.27: “How long will this wicked congregation grumble against Me? I have heard the complaints that the Israelites (God prevails) are making against Me.
14.28: So tell them: As surely as I live, declares the LORD {YHWH}, I will do to you? exactly as I heard you? say.
14.29: Your? bodies will fall in this wilderness—all who were numbered in the census, everyone twenty years of age or older—because you? have grumbled against Me.
14.30: Surely none of you? will enter the land in which I swore to settle you?, except Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) and Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity).
14.31: But I will bring your? children, whom you? said would become plunder, into the land you? have rejected—and they will enjoy it.
14.32: As for you?, however, your? bodies will fall in this wilderness.
14.33: Your? children will be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and they will suffer for your? unfaithfulness until the last of your? bodies lies in the wilderness.
14.34: In keeping with the forty days you? spied out the land, you? shall bear your? guilt forty years—a year for each day—and you? will experience My alienation.
14.35: I, the LORD {YHWH}, have spoken, and I will surely do these things to this entire wicked congregation, which has conspired against Me. They will meet their end in the wilderness, and there they will die.
14.36: So the men Moses (drawn out) had sent to spy out the land, who had returned and made the whole congregation grumble against him by bringing out a bad report about the land—
14.37: those men who had brought out the bad report about the land—were struck down by a plague before the LORD {YHWH}.
14.38: Of those men who had gone to spy out the land, only Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) and Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) remained alive.
14.39: And when Moses (drawn out) relayed these words to all the Israelites (God prevails), the people mourned bitterly.
14.40: Early the next morning they got up and went up toward the ridge of the hill country. “We have indeed sinned, they said, “but we will go to the place the LORD {YHWH} has promised.
14.41: But Moses (drawn out) said, “Why are you? transgressing the commandment of the LORD {YHWH}? This will not succeed!
14.42: Do not go up, lest you? be struck down by your? enemies, because the LORD {YHWH} is not among you?.
14.43: For there the Amalekites (dwellers in a valley) and Canaanites (merchants) will face you?, and you? will fall by the sword. Because you? have turned away from the LORD {YHWH}, He will not be with you?.
14.44: But they dared to go up to the ridge of the hill country, though neither Moses (drawn out) nor the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} moved from the camp.
14.45: Then the Amalekites (dwellers in a valley) and Canaanites (merchants) who lived in that part of the hill country came down, attacked them, and routed them all the way to Hormah (destruction).

15.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
15.2: “Speak to the Israelites (God prevails) and tell them: After you? enter the land that I am giving you? as a home
15.3: and you? present a food offering to the LORD {YHWH} from the herd or flock to produce a pleasing aroma to the LORD—either a burnt offering or a sacrifice, for a special vow or freewill offering or appointed feast—
15.4: then the one presenting his offering to the LORD {YHWH} shall also present a grain offering of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour <2 qt / 2.2 l> mixed with a quarter hin of olive oil <0.97 qt / 0.92 l>.
15.5: With the burnt offering or sacrifice of each lamb, you are to prepare a quarter hin of wine as a drink offering.
15.6: With a ram you are to prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah <4 qt / 4.4 l> of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of olive oil <1.3 qt / 1.2 l>,
15.7: and a third of a hin of wine as a drink offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD {YHWH}.
15.8: When you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice to fulfill a vow or as a peace offering to the LORD {YHWH},
15.9: present with the bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour <6 qt / 6.6 l> mixed with half a hin of olive oil <1.9 qt / 1.8 l>.
15.10: Also present half a hin of wine as a drink offering. It is a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD {YHWH}.
15.11: This is to be done for each bull, ram, lamb, or goat.
15.12: This is how you? must prepare each one, no matter how many.
