31.9: The Israelites (God prevails) captured the Midianite (strife) women and their children, and they plundered all their herds, flocks, and goods.
31.10: Then they burned all the cities where the Midianites had lived, as well as all their encampments,
31.11: and carried away all the plunder and spoils, both people and animals.
31.12: They brought the captives, spoils, and plunder to Moses (drawn out), to Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, and to the congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God) at the camp on the plains of Moab (of his father), by the Jordan (flowing down) across from Jericho (city of palms).
31.13: And Moses (drawn out), Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, and all the leaders of the congregation went to meet them outside the camp.
31.14: But Moses (drawn out) was angry with the officers of the army—the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds—who were returning from the battle.
31.15: “Have you? spared all the women? he (drawn out) asked them.
31.16: “Look, these women caused the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God), through the counsel of Balaam (not of the people), to turn unfaithfully against the LORD {YHWH} at Peor (cleft), so that the plague struck the congregation of the LORD.
31.17: So now, kill all the boys, as well as every woman who has had relations with a man,
31.18: but spare for yourselves every girl who has never had relations with a man.
31.19: All of you who have killed a person or touched the dead are to remain outside the camp for seven days. On the third day and the seventh day you? are to purify both yourselves and your? captives.
31.20: And purify every garment and leather good, everything made of goat’s hair, and every article of wood.
31.21: Then Eleazar (God has helped) the priest said to the soldiers who had gone into battle, “This is the statute of the law which the LORD {YHWH} has commanded Moses (drawn out):
31.22: Only the gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead—
31.23: everything that can withstand the fire—must be put through the fire, and it will be clean. But it must still be purified with the water of purification. And everything that cannot withstand the fire must pass through the water.
31.24: On the seventh day you? are to wash your? clothes, and you? will be clean. After that you? may enter the camp.
31.25: The LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
31.26: “You and Eleazar (God has helped) the priest and the family heads of the congregation are to take a count of what was captured, both of man and beast.
31.27: Then divide the captives between the troops who went out to battle and the rest of the congregation.
31.28: Set aside a tribute for the LORD {YHWH} from what belongs to the soldiers who went into battle: one out of every five hundred, whether persons, cattle, donkeys, or sheep.
31.29: Take it from their half and give it to Eleazar (God has helped) the priest as an offering to the LORD {YHWH}.
31.30: From the Israelites’ (he wrestles with God) half, take one out of every fifty, whether persons, cattle, donkeys, sheep, or other animals, and give them to the Levites who keep charge of the tabernacle of the LORD {YHWH}.
31.31: So Moses (drawn out) and Eleazar (God has helped) the priest did as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses,
31.32: and this plunder remained from the spoils the soldiers had taken: 675,000 sheep,
31.35: and 32,000 women who had not slept with a man.
31.36: This was the half portion for those who had gone to war: 337,500 sheep,
31.37: including a tribute to the LORD {YHWH} of 675,
31.38: 36,000 cattle, including a tribute to the LORD {YHWH} of 72,
31.39: 30,500 donkeys, including a tribute to the LORD {YHWH} of 61,
31.40: and 16,000 people, including a tribute to the LORD {YHWH} of 32.
31.41: Moses (drawn out) gave the tribute to Eleazar (God has helped) the priest as an offering for the LORD {YHWH}, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
31.42: From the Israelites’ (he wrestles with God) half, which Moses (drawn out) had set apart from the men who had gone to war,
31.43: this half belonged to the congregation: 337,500 sheep,
31.47: From the Israelites’ (he wrestles with God) half, Moses (drawn out) took one out of every fifty persons and animals and gave them to the Levites who kept charge of the tabernacle of the LORD {YHWH}, as the LORD had commanded him.
31.48: Then the officers who were over the units of the army—the commanders of thousands and of hundreds—approached Moses (drawn out)
31.49: and said (drawn out), “Your servants have counted the soldiers under our command, and not one of us is missing.
31.50: So we have brought to the LORD {YHWH} an offering of the gold articles each man acquired—armlets, bracelets, rings, earrings, and necklaces—to make atonement for ourselves before the LORD.
31.51: So Moses (drawn out) and Eleazar (God has helped) the priest received from them all the articles made out of gold.
31.52: All the gold that the commanders of thousands and of hundreds presented as an offering to the LORD {YHWH} weighed 16,750 shekels <420.8 lb / 190.9 kg>.
31.53: Each of the soldiers had taken plunder for himself.
31.54: And Moses (drawn out) and Eleazar (God has helped) the priest received the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds and brought it into the Tent of Meeting as a memorial for the Israelites (God prevails) before the LORD {YHWH}.

32.1: Now the Reubenites (behold a son) and Gadites (troop), who had very large herds and flocks, surveyed the lands of Jazer (helped) and Gilead (rocky region), and they saw that the region was suitable for livestock.
32.2: So the Gadites (troop) and Reubenites (behold a son) came to Moses (drawn out), Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, and the leaders of the congregation, and said,
32.3: “Ataroth (crowns), Dibon (wasting), Jazer (helped), Nimrah (pure), Heshbon (stronghold), Elealeh (God is ascending), Sebam [Sibmah], Nebo (prophet), and Beon (in the dwelling),
32.4: which the LORD {YHWH} conquered before the congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God), are suitable for livestock—and your servants have livestock.
32.5: “If we have found favor in your sight, they said, “let this land be given to your servants as a possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan (flowing down).
32.6: But Moses (drawn out) asked the Gadites (troop) and Reubenites (behold a son), “Shall your? brothers go to war while you? sit here?
32.7: Why are you? discouraging the Israelites (God prevails) from crossing into the land that the LORD {YHWH} has given them?
32.8: This is what your? fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea (holy) to inspect the land.
32.9: For when your fathers went up to the Valley of Eshcol (cluster) and saw the land, they discouraged the Israelites (God prevails) from entering the land that the LORD {YHWH} had given them.
32.10: So the anger of the LORD {YHWH} was kindled that day, and He swore an oath, saying,
32.11: ‘Because they did not follow Me wholeheartedly, not one of the men twenty years of age or older who came out of Egypt (land of bondage) will see the land that I swore to give Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he laughs), and Jacob (he grasps the heel)—
32.12: not one except Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) the Kenizzite and Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity)—because they did follow the LORD {YHWH} wholeheartedly.
32.13: The anger of the LORD {YHWH} burned against Israel (he wrestles with God), and He made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until the whole generation who had done evil in His sight was gone.
32.14: Now behold, you, a brood of sinners, have risen up in place of your? fathers to further stoke the burning anger of the LORD {YHWH} against Israel (he wrestles with God).
32.15: For if you? turn away from following Him, He will once again leave this people in the wilderness, and you? will be the cause of their destruction.
32.16: Then the Gadites and Reubenites approached Moses and said, “We want to build sheepfolds here for our livestock and cities for our little ones.
32.17: But we will arm ourselves and be ready [we will arm ourselves for battle] to go ahead of the Israelites (God prevails) until we have brought them into their place. Meanwhile, our little ones will remain in the fortified cities for protection from the inhabitants of the land.
32.18: We will not return to our homes until every Israelite (he wrestles with God) has taken possession of his inheritance.
32.19: Yet we will not have an inheritance with them across the Jordan (flowing down) and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on the east side of the Jordan.
32.20: Moses (drawn out) replied, “If you? will do this—if you? will arm yourselves before the LORD {YHWH} for battle,
32.21: and if every one of your? armed men crosses the Jordan (flowing down) before the LORD {YHWH}, until He has driven His enemies out before Him,
32.22: then when the land is subdued before the LORD {YHWH}, you? may return and be free of obligation to the LORD and to Israel (he wrestles with God). And this land will belong to you? as a possession before the LORD.
32.23: But if you? do not do this, you? will certainly sin against the LORD {YHWH}—and be assured that your? sin will find you? out.
32.24: Build cities for your? little ones and folds for your? flocks, but do what you? have promised.
32.25: The Gadites (troop) and Reubenites (behold a son) said to Moses (drawn out), “Your servants will do just as our lord commands.
32.26: Our children, our wives, our livestock, and all our animals will remain here in the cities of Gilead (rocky region).
32.27: But your servants are equipped for war, and every man will cross over to the battle before the LORD {YHWH}, just as our lord says.
32.28: So Moses (drawn out) gave orders about them to Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, to Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), and to the family leaders of the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God).
32.29: And Moses (drawn out) said to them, “If the Gadites (troop) and Reubenites (behold a son) cross the Jordan (flowing down) with you?, with every man armed for battle before the LORD {YHWH}, and the land is subdued before you?, then you? are to give them the land of Gilead (rocky region) as a possession.
32.30: But if they do not arm themselves and go across with you?, then they must accept their possession among you? in the land of Canaan (lowland).
32.31: The Gadites (troop) and Reubenites (behold a son) replied, “As the LORD {YHWH} has spoken to your servants, so we will do.
32.32: We will cross over into the land of Canaan (lowland) armed before the LORD {YHWH}, that we may have our inheritance on this side of the Jordan (flowing down).
32.33: So Moses (drawn out) gave to the Gadites (troop), to the Reubenites (behold a son), and to the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) son of Joseph (YHWH has added) the kingdom of Sihon (warrior) king of the Amorites (sayers) and the kingdom of Og (long-necked) king of Bashan (fruitful)—the land including its cities and the territory surrounding them.
32.34: And the Gadites (troop) built up Dibon (wasting), Ataroth (crowns), Aroer (ruins),
32.36: Beth-nimrah (house of the leopard), and Beth-haran (house of their mount) as fortified cities, and they built folds for their flocks.
32.37: The Reubenites (behold a son) built up Heshbon (stronghold), Elealeh (God is ascending), Kiriathaim (two cities),
32.38: as well as Nebo (prophet) and Baal-meon (lord of the habitation) (whose names were changed), and Sibmah (fragrance). And they renamed the cities they rebuilt.
32.39: The descendants of Machir (sold) son of Manasseh (causing to forget) went to Gilead (rocky region), captured it, and drove out the Amorites (sayers) who were there.
32.40: So Moses (drawn out) gave Gilead (rocky region) to the clan of Machir (sold) son of Manasseh (causing to forget), and they settled there.
32.41: Jair (he enlightens), a descendant of Manasseh (causing to forget), went and captured their villages and called them (villages of Jair) Havvoth-jair.
32.42: And Nobah (barking) went and captured Kenath (possession) and its villages and called it Nobah, after his own name.

33.1: These are the journeys of the Israelites (God prevails) when they came out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage) by their divisions under the leadership of Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer).
33.2: At the LORD’s {YHWH} command, Moses (drawn out) recorded the stages of their journey. These are the stages listed by their starting points:
33.3: On the fifteenth day of the first month, on the day after the Passover, the Israelites set out from Rameses (child of the sun). They (he wrestles with God) marched out defiantly (marched out boldly) in full view of all the Egyptians (double straits),
33.4: who (double straits) were burying all their firstborn, whom the LORD {YHWH} had struck down among them; for the LORD had executed judgment against their gods.
33.5: The Israelites (God prevails) set out from Rameses (child of the sun) and camped at Succoth (booths).
33.6: They set out from Succoth (booths) and camped at Etham (with them: their plowshare), on the edge of the wilderness.
33.7: They set out from Etham (with them: their plowshare) and turned back to Pi-hahiroth (place where sedge grows), opposite Baal-zephon (lord of the north), and they camped near Migdol (tower).
33.8: They set out from Pi-hahiroth [Hahiroth] and crossed through the sea, into the wilderness, and they journeyed three days into the Wilderness of Etham (with them: their plowshare) and camped at Marah (bitter).
33.9: They set out from Marah (bitter) and came to Elim (palms), where there were twelve springs and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.
33.10: They set out from Elim (palms) and camped by the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds).
33.11: They set out from the Red Sea and camped in the Desert of Sin (thorn).
33.12: They set out from the Desert of Sin (thorn / clay) and camped at Dophkah (knocking).
33.13: They set out from Dophkah (knocking) and camped at Alush (I will knead bread).
33.14: They set out from Alush (I will knead bread) and camped at Rephidim (resting places), where there was no water for the people to drink.
33.15: They set out from Rephidim (resting places) and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny).
33.16: They set out from the Wilderness of Sinai (thorny) and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah (graves of craving).
33.17: They set out from Kibroth-hattaavah (graves of craving) and camped at Hazeroth (settlement).
33.18: They set out from Hazeroth (settlement) and camped at Rithmah (heath).
33.19: They set out from Rithmah (heath) and camped at Rimmon-perez (pomegranate of the breaking out).
33.20: They set out from Rimmon-perez (pomegranate of the breaking out) and camped at Libnah (pavement).
33.21: They set out from Libnah (pavement) and camped at Rissah (ruin).
33.22: They set out from Rissah (ruin) and camped at Kehelathah (assembly).
33.23: They set out from Kehelathah (assembly) and camped at Mount Shepher (beauty).
33.24: They set out from Mount Shepher (beauty) and camped at Haradah (fear).
33.25: They set out from Haradah (fear) and camped at Makheloth (place of assembly).
33.26: They set out from Makheloth (place of assembly) and camped at Tahath (station).
33.27: They set out from Tahath (station) and camped at Terah.
33.28: They set out from Terah (station) and camped at Mithkah (sweetness).
33.29: They set out from Mithkah (sweetness) and camped at Hashmonah (fatness).
33.30: They set out from Hashmonah (fatness) and camped at Moseroth (bonds).
33.31: They set out from Moseroth (bonds) and camped at Bene-jaakan (sons of twisting).
33.32: They set out from Bene-jaakan (sons of twisting) and camped at Hor-haggidgad (cavern of Gidgad).
33.33: They set out from Hor-haggidgad (cavern of Gidgad) and camped at Jotbathah (pleasantness).
33.34: They set out from Jotbathah (pleasantness) and camped at Abronah (passage).
33.35: They set out from Abronah (passage) and camped at Ezion-geber (backbone).
33.36: They set out from Ezion-geber (backbone) and camped at Kadesh (holy) in the Wilderness of Zin (flat).
33.37: They set out from Kadesh (holy) and camped at Mount Hor (mountain), on the outskirts of the land of Edom (red).
33.38: At the LORD’s {YHWH} command, Aaron (light bringer) the priest climbed Mount Hor (mountain) and died there on the first day of the fifth month, in the fortieth year after the Israelites (God prevails) had come out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage).
33.39: Aaron (light bringer) was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor (mountain).
33.40: Now the Canaanite (merchant) king of Arad (a wild donkey), who lived in the Negev (southland) in the land of Canaan (lowland), heard that the Israelites (God prevails) were coming.
33.41: And the Israelites set out from Mount Hor (mountain) and camped at Zalmonah (shady).
33.42: They set out from Zalmonah (shady) and camped at Punon (darkness).
33.43: They set out from Punon (darkness) and camped at Oboth (waterskins).
33.44: They set out from Oboth (waterskins) and camped at Iye-abarim (ruins of Abarim) on the border of Moab (of his father).
33.45: They set out from Iyim (Iye-abarim) and camped at Dibon-gad (wasting) (troop).
33.46: They set out from Dibon-gad (wasting) (troop) and camped at Almon-diblathaim (concealing the two cakes).
33.47: They set out from (concealing the two cakes) Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim (the mountains beyond the river) facing Nebo (prophet).
33.48: They set out from the mountains of Abarim (regions beyond) and camped on the plains of Moab (of his father) by the Jordan (flowing down) across from Jericho (city of palms).
33.49: And there on the plains of Moab (of his father) they camped by the Jordan (flowing down), from Beth-jeshimoth (house of the desolation) to Abel-shittim (the Meadow of the Acacias).
33.50: On the plains of Moab (of his father) by the Jordan (flowing down) across from Jericho (city of palms), the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
33.51: “Speak to the Israelites (God prevails) and tell them: When you? cross the Jordan (flowing down) into the land of Canaan (lowland),
33.52: you? must drive out before you? all the inhabitants of the land, destroy all their carved images and cast idols, and demolish all their high places.
33.53: You? are to take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you? the land to possess.
33.54: And you? are to divide the land by lot according to your? clans. Give a larger inheritance to a larger clan and a smaller inheritance to a smaller one. Whatever falls to each one by lot will be his. You? will receive an inheritance according to the tribes of your? fathers.
33.55: But if you? do not drive out the inhabitants of the land before you?, those you? allow to remain will become barbs in your? eyes and thorns in your? sides; they will harass you? in the land where you? settle.
33.56: And then I will do to you? what I had planned to do to them.

34.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
34.2: “Command the Israelites (God prevails) and say to them: When you? enter the land of Canaan (lowland), it will be allotted to you? as an inheritance with these boundaries:
34.3: Your? southern border will extend from the Wilderness of Zin (flat) along the border of Edom (red). On the east, your? southern border will run from the end of the Salt Sea (the Dead Sea),
34.4: cross south of the Ascent (ascent of scorpions) of Akrabbim (the Ascent of Scorpions), continue to Zin (flat), and go south of Kadesh-barnea (holy). Then it will go on to Hazar-addar (enclosure of glory) and proceed to Azmon (strong),
34.5: where it will turn from Azmon (strong), join the Brook of Egypt (land of bondage), and end at the Sea (the Mediterranean Sea).
34.6: Your? western border will be the coastline of the Great Sea; this will be your? boundary on the west.
34.7: Your? northern border will run from the Great Sea directly to Mount Hor (mountain),
34.8: and from Mount Hor (mountain) to Lebo-hamath (fortress), then extend to Zedad (mountain side),
34.9: continue to Ziphron (fragrance), and end at Hazar-enan (village of fountains). This will be your? boundary on the north.
34.10: And your? eastern (village of fountains) border will run straight from Hazar-enan to Shepham (bald),
34.11: then go down from Shepham (bald) to Riblah (fertility) on the east side of Ain (spring) and continue along the slopes east of the Sea of Chinnereth (the Sea of Galilee).
34.12: Then the border will go down along the Jordan (flowing down) and end at the Salt Sea. This will be your? land, defined by its borders on all sides.
34.13: So Moses (drawn out) commanded the Israelites (God prevails), “Apportion this land by lot as an inheritance. The LORD {YHWH} has commanded that it be given to the nine and a half tribes.
34.14: For the tribes of the Reubenites and Gadites, (a troop / fortune) along with the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget), have already received their inheritance.
34.15: These two and a half tribes have received their inheritance across the Jordan (flowing down) from Jericho (city of palms), toward the sunrise.
34.16: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
34.17: “These are the names of the men who are to assign the land as an inheritance for you?: Eleazar (God has helped) the priest and Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity).
34.18: Appoint one leader from each tribe to distribute the land.
34.19: These are their names: Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) from the tribe of Judah (praised);
34.20: Shemuel (his name is El) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty) from the tribe of Simeon (heard);
34.21: Elidad (my God has loved) son of Chislon (confidence) from the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand);
34.22: Bukki (wasting) son of Jogli (he is exiled), a leader from the tribe of Dan (He has judged);
34.23: Hanniel (favored of God) son of Ephod, a leader from the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) son of Joseph (YHWH has added);
34.24: Kemuel (raised of God) son of Shiphtan (judicial), a leader from the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful);
34.25: Eli-zaphan (my God has protected) son of Parnach (delicate), a leader from the tribe of Zebulun (exalted);
34.26: Paltiel (God delivers) son of Azzan (very strong), a leader from the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense);
34.27: Ahihud (brother of majesty) son of Shelomi (peaceful), a leader from the tribe of Asher (happy);
34.28: and Pedahel (ransomed by God) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty), a leader from the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling).
34.29: These are the ones whom the LORD {YHWH} commanded to apportion the inheritance to the Israelites (God prevails) in the land of Canaan (lowland).

35.1: Again the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Moses (drawn out) on the plains of Moab (of his father) by the Jordan (flowing down) across from Jericho (city of palms):
35.2: “Command the Israelites (God prevails) to give, from the inheritance they will possess, cities for the Levites to live in and pasturelands around the cities.
35.3: The cities will be for them to live in, and the pasturelands will be for their herds, their flocks, and all their other livestock.
35.4: The pasturelands around the cities you? are to give the Levites will extend a thousand cubits <1,500 ft / 457.2 m> from the wall on every side.
35.5: You? are also to measure two thousand cubits <3,000 ft / 914.4 m> outside the city on the east, two thousand on the south, two thousand on the west, and two thousand on the north, with the city in the center. These areas will serve as larger pasturelands for the cities.
35.6: Six of the cities you? give the Levites are to be appointed as cities of refuge, to which a manslayer may flee. In addition to these, give the Levites forty-two other cities.
35.7: The total number of cities you? give the Levites will be forty-eight, with their corresponding pasturelands.
35.8: The cities that you? apportion from the territory of the Israelites (God prevails) should be given to the Levites in proportion to the inheritance of each tribe: more from a larger tribe and less from a smaller one.
35.9: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out),
35.10: “Speak to the Israelites (God prevails) and tell them: When you? cross the Jordan (flowing down) into the land of Canaan (lowland),
35.11: designate cities to serve as your? cities of refuge, so that a person who kills someone unintentionally may flee there.
35.12: You? are to have these cities as a refuge from the avenger, so that the manslayer will not die until he stands trial before the assembly.
35.13: The cities you? select will be your? six cities of refuge.
35.14: Select three cities across the Jordan (flowing down) and three in the land of Canaan (lowland) as cities of refuge.
35.15: These six cities will serve as a refuge for the Israelites (God prevails) and for the foreigner or stranger among them, so that anyone who kills a person unintentionally may flee there.
35.16: If, however, anyone strikes a person with an iron object and kills him, he is a murderer; the murderer must surely be put to death.
35.17: Or if anyone has in his hand a stone of deadly size, and he strikes and kills another, he is a murderer; the murderer must surely be put to death.
35.18: If anyone has in his hand a deadly object of wood, and he strikes and kills another, he is a murderer; the murderer must surely be put to death.
35.19: The avenger of blood is to put the murderer to death; when he finds him, he is to kill him.
35.20: Likewise, if anyone maliciously pushes another or intentionally throws an object at him and kills him,
35.21: or if in hostility he strikes him with his hand and he dies, the one who struck him must surely be put to death; he is a murderer. When the avenger of blood finds the murderer, he is to kill him.
35.22: But if anyone pushes a person suddenly, without hostility, or throws an object at him unintentionally,
35.23: or without looking drops a heavy stone that kills him, but he was not an enemy and did not intend to harm him,
35.24: then the congregation must judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood according to these ordinances.
35.25: The assembly is to protect the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood. Then the assembly will return him to the city of refuge to which he fled, and he must live there until the death of the high priest, who was anointed with the holy oil.
35.26: But if the manslayer ever goes outside the limits of the city of refuge to which he fled
35.27: and the avenger of blood finds him outside of his city of refuge and kills him, then the avenger will not be guilty of bloodshed,
35.28: because the manslayer must remain in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may he return to the land he owns.
35.29: This will be a statutory ordinance for you? for the generations to come, wherever you? live.
35.30: If anyone kills a person, the murderer is to be put to death on the testimony of the witnesses. But no one is to be put to death based on the testimony of a lone witness.
35.31: You? are not to accept a ransom for the life of a murderer who deserves to die; he must surely be put to death.
35.32: Nor should you? accept a ransom for the person who flees to a city of refuge and allow him to return and live on his own land before the death of the high priest.
35.33: Do not pollute the land where you? live, for bloodshed pollutes the land, and no atonement can be made for the land on which the blood is shed, except by the blood of the one who shed it.
35.34: Do not defile the land where you? live and where I dwell. For I, the LORD {YHWH}, dwell among the Israelites (God prevails).

36.1: Now the family heads of the clan of Gilead (rocky region) son of Machir (sold) son of Manasseh (causing to forget), one of the clans of Joseph (YHWH has added), approached Moses (drawn out) and the leaders who were the heads of the Israelite families (he wrestles with God) and addressed them,
36.2: saying, “When the LORD {YHWH} commanded my lord to give the land as an inheritance to the Israelites (God prevails) by lot, He also commanded him to give the inheritance of our brother Zelophehad (first-born) to his daughters.
36.3: But if they marry any of the men from the other tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God), their inheritance will be withdrawn from the portion of our fathers and added to the tribe into which they marry. So our allotted inheritance would be taken away.
36.4: And when the Jubilee for the Israelites (God prevails) comes, their inheritance will be added to the tribe into which they marry and taken away from the tribe of our fathers.
36.5: So at the word of the LORD {YHWH}, Moses (drawn out) commanded the Israelites (God prevails): “The tribe of the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added) speaks correctly.
36.6: This is what the LORD {YHWH} has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad (first-born): They may marry anyone they please, provided they marry within a clan of the tribe of their father.
36.7: No inheritance in Israel (he wrestles with God) may be transferred from tribe to tribe, because each of the Israelites is to retain the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.
36.8: Every daughter who possesses an inheritance from any Israelite (he wrestles with God) tribe must marry within a clan of the tribe of her father, so that every Israelite will possess the inheritance of his fathers.
36.9: No inheritance may be transferred from one tribe to another, for each tribe of Israel (he wrestles with God) must retain its inheritance.
36.10: So the daughters of Zelophehad (first-born) did as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out).
36.11: Mahlah (disease), Tirzah (favorable), Hoglah (partridge), Milcah (queen), and Noah (motion), the daughters of Zelophehad (first-born), were married to cousins on their father’s side.
36.12: They married within the clans of the descendants of Manasseh (causing to forget) son of Joseph (YHWH has added), and their inheritance remained within the tribe of their father’s clan.
36.13: These are the commandments and ordinances that the LORD {YHWH} gave the Israelites (God prevails) through Moses (drawn out) on the plains of Moab (of his father) by the Jordan (flowing down) across from Jericho (city of palms).
